Expose a single public GasToken::balance_of(snapshot, account)->BigInt from the natural owner (neo-native-contracts) and have neo-mempool and neo-rpc call it. Medium risk because it touches the balance-decode used on the verification path — confirm byte-equivalence with existing tests.

prioritized_actions.title: Remove dead interop-descriptor framework and unit-inconsistent legacy gas accounting in neo-execution
prioritized_actions.why: InteropDescriptor/InteropParameterDescriptor are a complete parallel registration framework with zero production callers (live dispatch uses register_host_service+HostInteropHandler). Separately consume_gas/check_gas/add_gas mutate picoGAS gas_consumed against datoshi gas_limit — an off-by-FEE_FACTOR unit mismatch — safe ONLY because all three have zero callers; latent correctness trap for the next caller. Delete both the descriptor files (+lib.rs re-exports) and the three legacy gas fns (delegate to add_fee_datoshi if anything is needed). No protocol path touched.

prioritized_actions.title: Trim neo-vm parallel gas-metering + never-constructed VmError variants (wrong-layer weight)
prioritized_actions.why: The engine carries gas_consumed/gas_limit/DEFAULT_GAS_LIMIT and 8 accessors but the loop never enforces gas_limit (only max_instructions) and only add_gas_consumed has one external caller — real metering is in ApplicationEngine. VmError has ~12 never-constructed higher-layer variants (ExecutionTimeout/ContractNotFound/MemoryLimitExceeded/CallDepthLimitExceeded/InvalidWitness/VmFault...) plus their constructors. Reduce the gas surface to what ApplicationEngine needs and prune VmError to what the VM actually emits. Does not change protocol-locked VM semantics.

prioritized_actions.title: Remove dead neo-network TaskManagerService + unused typed-wire layer
prioritized_actions.affected: neo-network/src/task_manager.rs (315 LoC, no-op); wire/protocol_message.rs + network_message.rs (~380 LoC); re-exports lib.rs:136-142
prioritized_actions.why: TaskManagerService is a fully-public command/handle/service skeleton with no-op handlers and no production caller (real sync is remote_node.rs::request_blocks_if_behind); its own doc admits scheduling is deferred. ProtocolMessage/NetworkMessage implement a second decode/encode path for every command but the hot path (on_message) uses manual per-command deserialize — they're used nowhere outside their modules. Either adopt the typed layer in on_message and delete the manual dispatch, or delete the typed layer; and delete/privatize task_manager. Keeping public no-op orchestrators invites misuse. No wire bytes change.

prioritized_actions.title: Replace neo-tee FcfsWithGasCap dead-logic gas cap (overflow panic + no-op)
prioritized_actions.why: `network_fee.min(network_fee * mult)` is a no-op for the intended case (mult>=1 => product>=original) so the gas cap never applies, AND `network_fee * mult` can overflow i64 for attacker-chosen network_fee (from untrusted tx input via add_transaction), panicking in debug. The stated anti-gas-war policy is unimplemented. At minimum use saturating_mul to remove the panic; ideally cap against a median reference and add a test that the cap actually changes ordering. neo-tee is off the ledger/P2P path so risk is low.

prioritized_actions.title: Migrate stringly-typed public APIs to CoreResult per CONVENTIONS.md
prioritized_actions.affected: neo-manifest (method_token/nef_file/contract_method+event_descriptor/contract_abi/wild_card_container/contract_permission_descriptor), neo-serialization (binary_serializer/json_serializer), neo-wallets (bip32/version/WalletFactory), neo-system TxRouterHandle, neo-crypto NodeType::from_byte
prioritized_actions.why: Cross-boundary public APIs return Result<_,String>, against the layered-error strategy (CoreError/CoreResult across crates; crate-internal thiserror within). Forces ad-hoc .map_err at every call site and loses structure. Introduce crate-internal thiserror types (Bip32Error, SerializerError) reused from existing siblings (Bip39Error/JsonError) and expose CoreError at the boundary. Low risk, mechanical, but L-effort due to breadth — do per-crate.

prioritized_actions.title: Remove unused dependencies across crates (cargo-machete/udeps sweep)
prioritized_actions.affected: neo-node (mimalloc handled separately; prometheus/sysinfo/chrono/rand/url/zeroize/num_cpus/tracing-appender/async-trait + dead optional neo-* crates), neo-consensus (neo-system/neo-network/async-trait), neo-config (uuid/dirs/toml + error.rs), neo-runtime (futures/parking_lot/tracing), neo-tee (anyhow)
prioritized_actions.why: Confirmed-class across 5 crates: numerous declared deps have zero src references, bloating compile time and the dependency-audit surface and blurring the layering the consolidation aimed for (e.g. neo-consensus, a Layer-1 core crate, pulls the network/system graphs). Run cargo-machete/udeps, drop the unused deps, decide explicitly on dead optional neo-* crates. Verify each with `cargo build -p <crate>`.

prioritized_actions.title: Update stale neo-core / removed-type module docs and broken intra-doc links
prioritized_actions.affected: neo-network/src/lib.rs:18-22,33-36,73-82; neo-manifest/src/lib.rs:28-38 + manifest/mod.rs:6-16; neo-payloads/src/validation.rs:13-18 + verifiable_ext.rs:8-12; neo-runtime/src/lib.rs:27,43,44 + services.rs:30 (4 broken intra-doc links)
prioritized_actions.why: Module docs describe a dissolved neo-core / moved types as the canonical home, and neo-runtime has 4 broken intra-doc links to BlockchainHandle/BlockchainCommand (moved to neo-blockchain in d794bf35) that fail `cargo doc -D rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links`. Misleads readers and breaks any docs-CI that denies broken links. Mechanical doc edits.

prioritized_actions.title: Fix fictional/contradictory crate-level doc examples
prioritized_actions.affected: neo-config/src/lib.rs:16-24 (Settings::from_file — no such type, JSON not TOML); neo-hsm/src/lib.rs:23-38 (HsmRuntime — no such type); neo-system/src/node.rs:164-168 (cancellation_token doc contradicts shared-clone impl + misnamed test)
prioritized_actions.why: The first thing an API reader sees references types/methods that do not exist (all `ignore`d so doctests never caught them), or a doc contract that is the opposite of the implementation (cancellation_token claims an independent token but returns a shared clone). Rewrite examples against the real API and drop `ignore` so they compile-check; fix the cancellation_token doc + test name to shared-state semantics.

prioritized_actions.title: Avoid eager whole-store clone on MemorySnapshot (default in-memory provider)
prioritized_actions.why: MemorySnapshot::new deep-clones the entire backing BTreeMap on every snapshot() call (O(n)), and find() clones the whole map AGAIN per call to merge the write batch. MemoryStore is the default provider (registered for "" and "Memory"), so this hits every in-memory test/dev path and any per-block snapshot. Hold an Arc to the store map + overlay (read store-then-overlay like RocksDbSnapshot) or use a persistent map for O(1) snapshots; at minimum iterate immutable+overlay lazily in find(). No protocol bytes.

prioritized_actions.title: Give RocksDbSnapshot its own read cache (point-in-time isolation)
prioritized_actions.why: snapshot() passes self.read_cache.clone() (Arc to the SAME StorageReadCache) into the snapshot, which then caches its point-in-time reads into the shared cache — so a snapshot read can be served as stale by a later live store try_get and vice versa; cross-handle invalidation isn't covered. A cache-coherency hazard on the read path, mitigated in practice only because StoreCache::new_from_snapshot reads via the Vec<u8> path that skips read_cache (the bare StorageKey path is still wrong). Give each snapshot its own empty cache (or disable cache on snapshots); add a write-v1/snapshot/overwrite-v2/assert-isolation test. Note: rocksdb is an optional backend.

prioritized_actions.title: Remove the AutoFlushBatchBuffer detached never-terminating thread
prioritized_actions.why: AutoFlushBatchBuffer::new spawns a detached std::thread with an infinite loop, no shutdown signal and no JoinHandle, holding Arc<DB> alive forever and ignoring Drop — each constructed buffer leaks a thread and keeps the DB open. Unused today (only re-exported, never instantiated), so latent. Delete it (unused), or rework to hold a JoinHandle + shutdown flag joined on Drop with a test that dropping terminates the thread.

prioritized_actions.title: Drop dead/speculative modules in Layer-0/Layer-1 crates
prioritized_actions.affected: neo-primitives/src/big_decimal_bigdecimal.rs (Fixed8) + bigdecimal dep + constants.rs:142-146 dead VM-limit consts; neo-storage/src/persistence/index.rs (~293 LoC); neo-runtime services.rs:71-166 (BlockExecutor/ConsensusService/NeoEngine traits); neo-config hardfork global singleton + GenesisConfig
prioritized_actions.why: Multiple unused speculative subsystems in foundation crates: a private bigdecimal-backed Fixed8 (pulls the bigdecimal crate for nothing); dead VM-limit constants (MAX_ARRAY_SIZE/MAX_ITEM_SIZE set to MAX_BLOCK_SIZE — a latent consensus trap if wired to the VM); a generic secondary-index module with no consumer and a String error; three unimplemented service traits; a process-global runtime-mutable HardforkManager (determinism footgun) all bypassed by per-ProtocolSettings. Delete or feature-gate. The VM-limit constants are the highest-value here (consensus trap). No protocol bytes if deleted, but if values are kept they must match C# ExecutionEngineLimits.

prioritized_actions.title: Normalize PKCS#11 ECDSA signatures to low-s (HSM signing parity)
prioritized_actions.why: The PKCS#11 sign path returns the token's raw r||s without normalizing s to low-s; a token is free to emit high-s, a valid ECDSA sig that may fail Neo verification / diverge from C# parity. The rest of neo-crypto normalizes s. After obtaining r||s, normalize s to low-s before returning. Affects produced signature bytes on the protocol path -> add a differential test against C#/neo-crypto verification. neo-hsm is operator-facing but the bytes go on-chain.

prioritized_actions.title: Replace neo-tee hand-rolled Merkle root with neo-crypto MerkleTree
prioritized_actions.why: A second single-SHA256 Merkle implementation duplicates neo-crypto's MerkleTree (already a dep) and uses a different hash_pair convention, so the TEE ordering-proof root is silently a different construction than the rest of the codebase. Consolidate onto neo_crypto::MerkleTree::compute_root (map [u8;32]->UInt256, [0;32] for empty). This is a TEE ordering proof, not a consensus root, so consolidate first; add a differential test since the odd-leaf duplication pattern differs.
